theme.description: High-end fitness analytics dashboard reminiscent of Whoop/Strava with tactical minimalism and deep data storytelling. The UI feels like a command center, balancing stark data clarity with energetic accents.

scale.metric_label: text-[10px] sm:text-xs font-semibold uppercase tracking-[0.2em] text-[#959595]

layout_and_spacing.container: w-full max-w-[720px] mx-auto px-4 sm:px-6 pb-24 pt-6

cards.base: rounded-2xl bg-[#242424] border border-white/10 p-5 relative overflow-hidden
cards.glass: backdrop-blur-xl bg-[#242424]/80 border border-white/10 p-5 rounded-2xl
cards.metric_card: flex flex-col justify-between items-start col-span-1 rounded-2xl bg-[#242424] border border-white/5 hover:border-white/10 p-4 transition-colors

StatsScreen.header: Flex row, avatar (rounded-full, subtle #FF6B00 glow ring) + greeting + scope toggle (glass pill with active state using accent gradient).
StatsScreen.kpi_grid: Bento box style: Total Workouts, Streak, Avg/Week, Completion %, Total Tonnage, Time in Gym. Use 'metric_value' + 'metric_label'. Top cards span 2 columns, minor metrics span 1 column.
StatsScreen.1rm_cards: Horizontal scrolling or 3-column grid for Squat/Bench/Deadlift. Progress bar showing plan vs achieved.

charts.tonnage_bar: Recharts BarChart. Use 'radius={[4, 4, 0, 0]}' on bars. Fill with accent gradient. Add a 1px subtle grid line. Hide axis lines.
charts.exercise_line: Recharts LineChart for 1RM progress. Use monotone curve. Area under curve using SVG linearGradient from #FF6B00 at 50% opacity to 0%. strokeWidth={3}.
charts.muscle_donut: Recharts PieChart (donut). Inner radius 60%, outer 80%. Stroke #242424 (card bg) with strokeWidth={4} to create visual gaps between slices.
charts.plan_adherence: Stacked Bar chart or multi-progress bar indicating volume/schedule deviation.
charts.tooltips: Recharts Tooltip MUST have contentStyle={{ backgroundColor: '#1C1C1C', border: '1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.1)', borderRadius: '12px', color: '#fff', padding: '8px 12px', boxShadow: '0 8px 32px rgba(0,0,0,0.4)' }}.

StatsScreen.recent_sessions: List view. Each item is a glass card. Includes a mini circular progress ring (SVG or Recharts RadialBar) for volume completion.

StreakScreen.hero: Massive flame/streak number display. Use GG Zaglav font, text-6xl+ to text-8xl. Center aligned. The flame icon should have a CSS-only subtle pulse/drop-shadow glow.
StreakScreen.this_week: Row of 7 circles. Active days filled with gradient, upcoming empty with white/10 border. Current day has subtle glow and slightly larger scale.
StreakScreen.heatmap: GitHub-style, 12 columns (weeks) x 7 rows (days). Cells rounded-sm (2px to 4px). Utilize CSS Grid. Colors mapped from chart.heatmap tokens.

athlete_avatar.description: Athlete portrait avatar for header. Fallback if user avatar is null.

testing.attributes: All interactive elements, metric cards, chart wrappers, and list items MUST use data-testid attributes (e.g., data-testid='scope-toggle-plan', data-testid='streak-metric-card', data-testid='kpi-total-workouts').

instructions_to_main_agent: Ensure all Recharts charts are fully responsive using ResponsiveContainer with a defined aspect ratio or min-height (e.g., h-[300px] or aspect={2.5}).
Implement the 'Selected Day' gradient (linear-gradient(-34deg, #FF8A24, #FFDA24)) for active elements, but use it sparingly to maintain high contrast and premium feel.
Do NOT use pure black (#000000) - strictly use the #1C1C1C background and #242424 for cards to prevent OLED smearing and maintain the tactical aesthetic.
Use SVG <linearGradient> within Recharts <defs> for smooth area charts and glowing bar fills.
For the Heatmap, implement a pure CSS Grid (grid-cols-12 grid-rows-7) with gaps (gap-1 or gap-2). Avoid heavy charting libraries for the heatmap to ensure mobile performance.
Add 'data-testid' to all buttons, tabs, chart wrappers, and list items for automated testing.
Since framer-motion is prohibited, rely on Tailwind's native transition utilities (transition-all, duration-300, ease-out) for micro-interactions.
Files to be modified/created should be standard React .js and .jsx files. Do not use TypeScript (.tsx).
